text.title: pair-programming Failure Modes
text.summary: Three antipatterns each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: Passive navigator — silent while driver works alone.
antipattern.symptom: Pair becomes solo + spectator; no review value; navigator burns out.
antipattern.root-cause: No mode chosen / no enforcement of strong-style.
antipattern.fix: Re-state mode; rotate roles at next Pomodoro; in strong-style, navigator owns the next instruction.

antipattern.description: AI navigator dumps full files instead of one bounded instruction per turn.
antipattern.symptom: Human can't keep up; review value collapses; mistakes propagate unchecked.
antipattern.root-cause: Length pressure on LLM output; rules drift after ~10 turns.
antipattern.fix: Restate Strong-Style rules every 10 turns; cap output length; require "why" before each instruction.

antipattern.description: Switching modes mid-session (e.g. classic → strong-style halfway).
antipattern.symptom: Roles get muddy; both participants confused; retro can't characterise what happened.
antipattern.root-cause: Mode chosen ad hoc, no explicit reset.
antipattern.fix: Finish current Pomodoro; do a short reset (3 min): pick new mode, restart timer, log the switch in session.md.
